tagline: Offline clinical decision-support for community health workers — 100% on-device.
summary: Every inference (speech-to-text, MedPsy reasoning, embeddings/RAG, text-to-speech) runs locally via @qvac/sdk on the device's CPU/GPU. There are NO cloud AI calls and NO outbound network connections during operation. The patient's case never leaves the machine.

networkPolicy.duringInference: ZERO outbound connections. Proven from the code side by scripts/egress-check.ts and the automated test tests/integration/offline-egress.test.ts, which monkeypatch net.connect / tls.connect / dns.lookup / http(s).request / fetch and assert no external host is contacted during a full triage. A negative control in the same test proves the guard is not a no-op.
networkPolicy.duringDemo: Runs fully in airplane mode once the models are cached.
networkPolicy.inProcessSockets: The @qvac/sdk inference worker communicates with the app over a local UNIX-domain socket (IPC), which is not network egress.

disclosedNetworkEvents.frequency: one-time per machine (cached at ~/.qvac/models thereafter; skippable by pre-placing the GGUF/bin files)
disclosedNetworkEvents.purpose: Fetch the quantized model weights. These are weight downloads only — ZERO AI inference is performed remotely; all inference is local.

dataSources.name: WHO IMCI Chart Booklet (March 2014)
dataSources.use: Childhood-illness triage protocols (RAG corpus). Born-digital, text-extractable, ingested verbatim.

verification.zeroEgress: node --import tsx scripts/egress-check.ts   (prints ZERO outbound connections, exit 0)
verification.perfLog: submission/perf-log.csv + submission/perf-log.json — real on-device TTFT / tokens-per-sec / load-unload rows
verification.reproduce: npm install --maxsockets=1  →  npm run ingest  →  npm start  →  open http://localhost:3010
